title: 2026-27 California GovernorÃ¢â‚¬â„¢s Budget Trailer Bill Language: Sustainable Aviation Fuel Tax Credit
content: Trailer Bill 1365 amends the California Revenue and Taxation Code to create a state tax credit for sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) produced and sold in California, effective January 1, 2026. The bill allows a tax credit against state fuel taxes for qualifying SAF producers, providing $1 per gallon of SAF that meets defined carbon intensity reduction criteria, with the credit increasing by $0.02 per gallon for each additional 1% reduction in emissions below 50% of conventional jet fuel emissions. The credit applies only to SAF produced by the taxpayer claiming it and sold for use within California. It can be claimed on tax returns filed on or after November 1, 2027 for the preceding period, effectively incentivising production and in-state sales of lower-carbon aviation fuel.
author: Analysts Account
